title: Splinting (Basic Limb)
content_markdown:
#### Indications
- For the comfort and support of limb fractures, or soft tissue injuries.

#### Procedure
- If required, administer pain relief prior to applying splint.
- Confirm distal pulses are present.
- Realign limb if perfusion is compromised.
##### Cardboard Splint

- Select appropriate cardboard splint size, trauma combines or a towel for padding, and a roller bandage.

- Support the patient’s injured limb and position on cardboard splint and padding so that fingers/toes are at the rounded end (see **Figures 1 & 2**).

Figure 1

Figure 2

- Fold up sides of cardboard splint and secure with roller bandage and tape roller bandage in place with the help of a second person.

- Reconfirm distal pulses are present, sensation, and capillary refill.

##### Anatomical Leg Splint

- Place padding in between legs at level of any bony prominences (knees and ankles).

- Secure legs together with triangular bandage at proximal, middle, and distal regions appropriate to injury (see **Figure 3**).

Figure 3

- Reconfirm distal pulses are present, sensation, and capillary refill.
